summary: High‑energy show with soaring Jerry solos and killer Scarlet>Eyes
blurb: The band delivers a tight first set that builds into an adventurous second set, highlighted by the blistering Scarlet Begonias → Eyes of the World transition and a nonstop jam through The Wheel, Truckin' and The Other One. Jerry’s guitar work is described as “heavenly” throughout, while Brent adds trippy keyboard textures.
review: On April 27 1985 at Frost Amphitheatre, the Dead were firing on all cylinders. A crisp sound‑board mix captures Jerry Garcia in peak form – his solos are repeatedly called “orgasmic,” “heavenly,” and “flawless,” especially during the iconic Scarlet Begonias → Eyes of the World sequence and the soaring solo on Ramble On Rose. Phil Lesh lays down massive bass bombs that sit front‑and‑center, while Brent Mydland’s keys add a bright, trippy buzz that lifts the jams. The rhythm section—Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann—delivers honed chops and tight interplay, making the Drums/Space segment a highlight rather than a filler. Standout moments include the Tom Thumb > Cold Rain > Music mini‑suite, the high‑octane run through The Wheel → Truckin' → The Other One, and the sing‑along energy of Dancin' In The Street and Bertha. Across dozens of recordings the consensus is clear: this Frost show is a must‑listen snapshot of the mid‑80s Dead at their most exuberant.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All thirteen recordings report positive sentiment, repeatedly praising Jerry's soaring solos, the Scarlet Begonias → Eyes of the World transition, and tight band interplay; no negative comments were found, indicating strong consensus.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source described as crisp, punchy and pristine; captures the full energy of the show with no drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Jerry’s “heavenly” solo on Scarlet Begonias → Eyes of the World
High‑octane jam through The Wheel → Truckin' → The Other One
Seamless Tom Thumb > Cold Rain > Music mini‑suite
Tight drums/space segment that adds intensity to the second set

band_performance.Jerry: Flawless, soaring guitar tone with fire‑like solos; vocals strained at times but overall a “heavenly” performance highlighted by the iconic Scarlet>Eyes and Ramble On Rose moments.
band_performance.Phil: Great bass bombs and solid low‑end foundation; Modulus bass work shines, sitting high in the mix and driving the jams forward.
band_performance.Bob: Provides growling vocal texture and solid rhythm work on Truckin' and The Other One; slide solo on Rooster (not in set) deemed passable but overall supportive.
band_performance.Brent: Nice touch on keys with trippy bells and lingering buzzers that enhance the improvisations throughout both sets.
band_performance.Mickey: Honored chops and tight interplay with Billy, delivering an excellent Drums/Space segment that adds intensity to the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, responsive drumming that locks in with Mickey; together they create a driving rhythmic backbone for the high‑energy jams.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
